title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 9th February 2026 | TV5 News

recommendations.note: Hold; one of top-3 power cos, scope to 250 once 182 crosses
recommendations.detail: Hold Adani Power, no need to hurry as it is one of the top-three power companies. The recent correction eliminated weaker players in the stock. Once it crosses the recent high of 182, there is scope to go up to 250 on the way. Definitely hold for the next one year.

recommendations.note: Buy on dip near 110; good results, broking recovery play
recommendations.detail: Groww announced good December-quarter numbers. As markets recover, brokerage companies' incomes rise and a gung-ho atmosphere returns. Buy it when it comes closer to 110; looks good for at least a positional trade if not a long-term hold.

recommendations.note: Buy near 110; auto-ancillary, stock split done, liquidity up
recommendations.detail: Rolex Rings is fine; it did a stock split and liquidity increased. Buy it only when it comes closer to 110. It is an auto-ancillary company, not comparable to broking names like Groww.

recommendations.note: Hold with strict stop-loss; exit on rally to 150-156
recommendations.detail: December bottom line stayed flat despite top-line growth because costs rose on management-contract hotels with pricing revision; should absorb it and do better in the coming holiday season. Already corrected ~30% from 170-180 highs. Hold at current levels with a strict stop-loss between 110 and 150, but in the short term exit on a rally to 150-156.

recommendations.note: Buy; good Q3 numbers, must re-win KAVACH orders
recommendations.detail: Numbers are good though not comparable to the exceptional Q2 base. Q3 numbers are good and year-on-year performance was good. Going forward it must win KAVACH orders again in competition with Kernex and Concord Controls to strengthen its order book. After listening to the con-call it feels buyable.

recommendations.note: Buy/accumulate, no need to wait for a fall; add on dips
recommendations.detail: Buy SBI 100%, no need to look back and forth. Recommended repeatedly for almost three years, including shifting out of Yes Bank. If bought at the high and it dips, average it. In the long term SBI will cross the 1,500 level and stabilize; it is a bank, no doubt. Accumulate and keep adding on dips.

recommendations.note: Avoid at current levels after 200% 6-month run
recommendations.detail: The stock rose 200% in just six months (about 80 to 280) on speculation and is at 52-week highs. There are doubts it can rise further from these levels unless the company delivers another successful blockbuster VFX project. As of now do not buy; consider only on a strong decline.

recommendations.note: Wait another quarter; weak Dec result, micro-cap
recommendations.detail: Recently listed with only one quarter of performance, and the December result was not good at all, leaving the stock near its 52-week low around 130. Fundamentals do not seem to justify the valuation. It is a micro-cap (about Rs 850 crore market cap). Wait for another quarter.

recommendations.note: Buy for short/short-medium term only, not long term
recommendations.detail: The result has come and is good, but the stock is not a fancy one. Keeping that in view, it can be bought for the short or short-medium term. Long-term bets in such companies do not work well.

recommendations.note: Hold; result slightly below est but strong execution pipeline
recommendations.detail: The result came slightly below expectations but there are many projects in execution, so better results will come in coming quarters. Definitely hold; a little (heavy) patience is needed.

recommendations.note: Hold/stay invested; worst over, govt infra spend tailwind
recommendations.detail: The worst is over and the group's debt situation is stabilizing. As infra companies, do not sell as of now even if weak, because Government of India infra/GSDP-growth spending will certainly be high in the coming year. It is near its 52-week low and on a weak wicket, but should do much better from current levels.

recommendations.note: Wait; buy near major support 380-390 on weekly weakness
recommendations.detail: At current levels (around 420-430) do not buy; wait. Recommended at 60-70 rupees three years back, it touched 500. Major support is around 380-390. Buy when that level comes during weekly weakness.

recommendations.note: Hold; weak-looking but high potential, not cheap
recommendations.detail: Hold; no need to fear. Performance may feel weak but potential is high, and such future-discounting stocks tend to move. It is not a cheap stock, around Rs 12,000 crore market cap. The December profit dip was a one-off, so no need to exit even if the stock is weak now.

recommendations.note: Average; earnings-call developments could take it past 500
recommendations.detail: Average here. There are a couple of developments in the company that were indicated on the earnings/conference call. If those play out, there are signs the stock will cross 500 again in the next two-to-three quarters.
Hold and buy. Earlier advised to accumulate even at 320-330, with a medium-term target of 425-430. Definitely a buy.

recommendations.note: Hold; metal cycle stabilizing, good recent result
recommendations.detail: The metal cycle is stabilizing again and the recent result was good. Hold.
After the recent result it corrected and is stabilizing between 360-370. Definitely hold; buy too when it comes closer to 355.

recommendations.note: Avoid as investment; now a trading/F&O bet
recommendations.detail: It has become an institutional stock since going into F&O, with swift 5-10% weekly moves on and off. It has become more of a trading bet than an investment bet as of now.
